rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in the component type scope when they describe interface behavior, and implementation-level assign clauses in the matching implementation.
Use exact visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not invent or copy identifiers from examples.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be scoped to their owning component declaration.
Component type contracts may only reference features visible at that type scope.
Implementation-level bindings should use subcomponent outputs and ports valid in that scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference identifiers visible in the current scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless model exposes explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
